definition: The MOVE index serves as a useful but theoretically impure diagnostic for yield curve signal quality. MOVE <80 has historically corresponded to correct curve signals, MOVE >120 to false signals. The 2019 vs 2022-23 comparison provides a near-perfect natural experiment, but the framework may be partially circular since MOVE and term premium share yield curve inputs.
evidence: 2019 (MOVE <80) correct signal vs 2022-23 (MOVE >120) false signal—near-perfect natural experiment
pair_2 debate called this the 'strongest empirical contribution'
Current MOVE (~95-110) falls in diagnostically ambiguous middle zone
open_questions: Is the MOVE framework circular with term premium measurement?
Can survey-based inflation expectations or Blue Chip GDP forecasts provide a better independent diagnostic?
Are the thresholds (<80/80-120/>120) overfitted given derivation from essentially 5 observations?
